modules.title: One-to-One Correspondence (1-5)
modules.description: Learning to count each object once and only once.
modules.learningGoal: Master touching and counting objects in a set up to 5.

questions.question: How many suns are in the sky?
Count the tasty apples!
How many soccer balls do you see?
Can you count the blue cars?
High five! How many fingers are held up?
Which group shows exactly 2 stars?
questions.explanation: This group has one, two stars! ⭐⭐

questions.question: Touch the number 3!
questions.explanation: That is the number 3. It comes after 2!

questions.question: Are there more than 3 hearts here? ❤️❤️❤️❤️
questions.explanation: Yes! There are 4 hearts, and 4 is more than 3.

questions.question: How many ducks are swimming? 🦆🦆🦆
What is the next number? 1, 2, 3, __

modules.title: Number Recognition and Symbols (1-5)
modules.description: Matching the sound of a number to its written shape.
modules.learningGoal: Identify written digits 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.

questions.question: Which one of these is the number '1'?
questions.explanation: The number 1 looks like a straight line! 1

questions.question: Can you find the number '2'?
I have 3 cupcakes. Which number shows how many I have?
questions.explanation: There are 3 cupcakes, so we use the number 3!

questions.question: Look at these numbers: 1, 2, __, 4. What is the missing symbol?
questions.explanation: We count 1, 2, and then 3 comes next!

questions.question: Which number looks like a chair turned upside down?
questions.explanation: The number 4 often looks like a little chair!

questions.question: Match the digit to the word: 'Five'
questions.explanation: The word 'Five' belongs to the number 5.

questions.question: How many fingers are on one hand? Pick the right number.
questions.explanation: We have 5 fingers on one hand!

questions.question: Which number is 'Three'?
questions.explanation: Great job! That is the number 3.

questions.question: Is this the number 2? -> '5'
questions.explanation: No, that is the number 5. The number 2 looks like a swan!

questions.question: Count the ladybugs and find the number! 🐞🐞🐞🐞
questions.explanation: There are 4 ladybugs. We write that as 4.

modules.title: Counting Larger Groups (6-10)
modules.description: Applying counting rules to bigger sets.
modules.learningGoal: Accurately count groups up to 10.

questions.question: How many buzzing bees can you see?
questions.explanation: Count them: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5... and one more makes 6! 🐝

questions.question: Count the colorful balloons!
questions.explanation: There are 7 balloons in the bunch! 🎈

questions.question: How many legs does an octopus have?
questions.explanation: An octopus has 8 legs. Let's count them: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8!

questions.question: Count the yummy lollipops!
How many fingers do you have on TWO hands?
questions.explanation: 5 on one hand and 5 on the other makes 10 fingers! 🙌

questions.question: Which group has exactly 6 stars?
What number comes after 8?
questions.explanation: When we count, we say 7, 8, and then 9!

questions.question: Is this group 10? 🍩🍩🍩🍩🍩🍩🍩🍩🍩
questions.explanation: No, that is only 9 donuts. We need one more to make 10!

questions.question: Count the bouncing balls: ⚽⚽⚽⚽⚽⚽⚽
Find the number that shows a full set of ten!
questions.explanation: 10 is written with a 1 and a 0 together!

modules.title: Ordering and Sequences
modules.description: Arranging numbers from smallest to biggest.
modules.learningGoal: Complete number patterns up to 10.

questions.question: What number is missing? 1, 2, __, 4, 5
questions.explanation: When we count in order, 3 comes after 2 and before 4!

questions.question: Which number comes just BEFORE 2?
questions.explanation: We start with 1, then comes 2. So 1 is before 2!

questions.question: Help the train! What number comes AFTER 5? [3] [4] [5] [__]
questions.explanation: After 5, the next number we say is 6!

questions.question: Put these in order from smallest to biggest: 3, 1, 2
questions.explanation: We always start with the smallest number: 1, 2, 3!

questions.question: What number is missing? 6, 7, 8, __, 10
questions.explanation: The missing number is 9. It lives between 8 and 10!

questions.question: Which number is the biggest?
questions.explanation: 8 is the biggest because it comes last when we count these numbers.

questions.question: Can you count backwards? 3, 2, __
Which number is between 7 and 9?
questions.explanation: 7, 8, 9. The number 8 is right in the middle!

questions.question: What number comes just BEFORE 10?
questions.explanation: We say 9 just before we reach 10!

questions.question: What comes next in this pattern?
questions.explanation: This is counting in twos! 2, 4, 6, 8, and then 10.

modules.title: Comparing Quantities
modules.description: Using terms like 'more than', 'less than', or 'equal'.
modules.learningGoal: Compare two groups of objects visually.

questions.question: Which group has MORE?
questions.explanation: 4 apples is a bigger group than 2 apples!

questions.question: Which group has LESS?
Is 5 more than 2?
questions.explanation: Yes! When we count, 5 comes after 2, so it is more.

questions.question: Which number is the smallest?
questions.explanation: 3 is the smallest because it is the first one we say when counting.

questions.question: Are these groups equal (the same)? 🌟🌟 vs 🌟🌟
questions.explanation: They are equal! Both sides have exactly 2 stars.

questions.question: Which is LESS: 8 or 10?
Pick the group that is EQUAL to 5.
questions.explanation: A hand with all fingers up is exactly 5!

questions.question: Which number is GREATER than 7?
I have 4 cookies 🍪 and you have 4 cookies 🍪. Who has more?
questions.explanation: 4 and 4 are the same number, so we are equal!

questions.question: Which group is the BIGGEST?
questions.explanation: The group with 5 bees is the biggest one here!

modules.title: The Number Line (0-10)
modules.description: Visualizing numbers as points in space.
modules.learningGoal: Identify positions on a horizontal number line.

questions.question: Find the number 0 on the number line!
questions.explanation: We start at 0! It means there is nothing yet.

questions.question: A frog hops from 1 to the next number. Where does he land?
questions.explanation: One hop forward from 1 lands on 2!

questions.question: What number is missing on this line? 0, 1, 2, 3, __, 5
questions.explanation: The number 4 sits right between 3 and 5.

questions.question: Which number is closer to 0?
questions.explanation: 2 is closer to the start of our number line than 5 or 9.

questions.question: If you are at 5 and hop forward 1 time, where do you land?
questions.explanation: Moving forward means the numbers get bigger. 5 plus 1 is 6.

questions.question: Which number is at the very end of our line?
questions.explanation: On a 0 to 10 line, 10 is the last number!

questions.question: What number is missing? 5, 6, 7, __, 9, 10
questions.explanation: The number 8 comes after 7 and before 9.

questions.question: If you hop BACKWARDS from 3, what number do you land on?
questions.explanation: Hopping backwards makes the numbers smaller. Before 3 is 2.

questions.question: Is 7 further away from 0 than 3 is?
questions.explanation: Yes! You have to take more hops to get to 7 than to 3.

questions.question: Find the middle number between 0 and 10.
questions.explanation: 5 is right in the middle of our 0 to 10 number line!

modules.title: Number Names and Words
modules.description: Connecting digits to their South African English names.
modules.learningGoal: Match symbols (1) to words (one).

questions.question: Match the numbers to their names!
questions.explanation: Great job! 1 is One, 2 is Two, and 3 is Three.

questions.question: Match the bigger numbers to their names!
questions.explanation: Great job! 4 is Four, 5 is Five, and 6 is Six.

questions.question: Which word matches this group? 🍎🍎🍎
questions.explanation: There are 3 apples, and the word for 3 is 'Three'.

questions.question: How do we write the name for 4?
Match the word to the number: 'Five'
questions.explanation: High five! The word 'Five' is for the number 5.

questions.question: Which number word starts with the 'S' sound?
questions.explanation: Six starts with an 'S'. It is the word for 6.

questions.question: Count the stars and pick the word: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
questions.explanation: There are 7 stars. The word is 'Seven'.

questions.question: What is the name for the number 8?
Is 'Nine' the name for the number 9?
Pick the word for the biggest number in our lesson: 10

modules.title: Adding to 5
modules.description: Putting small groups together to make a bigger number.
modules.learningGoal: Add two numbers with a sum up to 5.

questions.question: 1 apple + 1 apple = how many apples? 🍎 + 🍎

questions.explanation: 2 + 1 = 3! Count on your fingers: 2... then 1 more makes 3!

questions.question: 1 + 2 = 3. Is this true?

questions.explanation: 3 + 2 = 5! Hold up 3 fingers, then 2 more. Count them all!
Adding zero changes nothing! 0 + 5 is still 5.

questions.question: Which sum equals 4?
Count the hearts: ❤️ + ❤️❤️❤️
questions.explanation: 1 heart plus 3 more hearts makes 4 hearts!

questions.question: What is 1 + 1 + 1?

modules.title: Adding Up to 10
modules.description: Learning to add bigger numbers using sets and number lines.
modules.learningGoal: Master basic addition sums with results from 6 to 10.

questions.explanation: 5 fingers on one hand and 1 on the other makes 6!

questions.question: Count the fish: 🐟🐟🐟 + 🐟🐟🐟🐟
Build the number 10 using two pieces!
Which sum equals 6?
Count the stars: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ + ⭐⭐⭐
Can you make 10 with these pieces?
questions.explanation: 9 and 1 more is 10! Also 4 and 6 make 10.

modules.title: Doubles and Near Doubles
modules.description: Learning that 1+1, 2+2, and 3+3 are special matches.
modules.learningGoal: Recall doubles up to 10 quickly.

questions.question: What is double 1? (1 + 1)
questions.explanation: Double 1 is 2! Think of your two eyes: 1 + 1 = 2.

questions.question: What is 2 + 2?
questions.explanation: 2 + 2 = 4. Like the four legs on a dog! 🐕

questions.question: If I double 3, what do I get? 🍎🍎🍎 + 🍎🍎🍎
Double 4 is 8. Is this true?
questions.explanation: Yes! 4 + 4 = 8. Think of a spider's 8 legs!

questions.question: What is the double of 5?
Look at this: 2 + 3. This is a 'Near Double'. What is the answer?
questions.explanation: It's almost double 2! 2 + 2 is 4, so 2 + 3 is just one more: 5.

questions.question: Which one of these is a 'Double'?
questions.explanation: A double is when both numbers are the same. 4 + 4 is a double!

questions.question: If 3 + 3 = 6, then what is 3 + 4?
questions.explanation: 3 + 4 is a near double. It is one more than 6, which is 7!

questions.question: Double __ = 4. What number is missing?
Finish the doubles pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, __
questions.explanation: These are the answers to our doubles: 1+1, 2+2, 3+3, 4+4... and 5+5 is 10!

modules.title: Addition Story Problems
modules.description: Solving real-life math stories.
modules.learningGoal: Solve simple word problems using addition.

questions.question: Thabo has 2 marbles. His friend gives him 1 more. How many marbles does Thabo have now?
There are 3 birds on a branch. 2 more birds fly to the branch. How many birds are there in total?
Sarah finds 4 shells on the beach. She finds 4 more. How many shells does she have?
questions.explanation: 4 shells + 4 shells is a double! That makes 8.

questions.question: A farm has 5 cows and 0 sheep. How many animals are on the farm?
You have 6 pencils. You buy 2 more. How many pencils do you have now?
Mom baked 3 cupcakes. Dad baked 3 cupcakes. How many cupcakes are there to eat?
A bus has 7 children. 3 more children get on. How many children are on the bus?
Lindiwe has 1 blue balloon and 4 red balloons. How many balloons does she have altogether?
There are 5 frogs in a pond. 4 more frogs jump in. How many frogs are in the pond?
If you have 2 sweets and I give you 2 more, you will have 4 sweets. Is this true?

modules.title: Adding with the Number Line
modules.description: Jumping forward on the line to find sums.
modules.learningGoal: Use a number line to add numbers up to 10.

questions.question: Start at 2. Jump forward 1 space. Where are you?
questions.explanation: Starting at 2 and taking 1 hop forward lands you on 3! 2 + 1 = 3.

questions.question: Start at 4. Take 2 jumps forward. [4] -> [5] -> [__]
To solve 5 + 3, you start at 5 and take how many jumps?
questions.explanation: You always start at the first number and jump the amount of the second number!

questions.question: Start at 7. Jump forward 3 times. What is the sum?
If you are at 0 and take 5 big jumps, where do you land?
Does 6 + 2 land on the same spot as 2 + 6?
questions.explanation: Yes! Both sums equal 8. It doesn't matter which number you start with!

questions.question: Start at 8. Jump forward 1. Where are you?
Start at 1. Jump forward 4. What is 1 + 4?
You are at 3. You want to get to 5. How many jumps do you need?
questions.explanation: From 3, you jump to 4, then to 5. That is 2 jumps!

questions.question: Which jump is bigger? 2 + 5 or 2 + 3?
questions.explanation: 2 + 5 lands on 7, which is further down the line than 5!

modules.title: Making 10
modules.description: Finding number pairs that equal 10.
modules.learningGoal: Identify all pairs (like 7+3 or 6+4) that make 10.

questions.question: I have 9. How many more do I need to make 10?
Which number is the partner for 5 to make 10?
questions.explanation: 5 + 5 = 10. Two full hands make a ten!

questions.question: 8 + __ = 10. What is the missing friend?
Does 7 + 3 make 10?
questions.explanation: Yes! 7 + 3 is a perfect way to make 10.

questions.question: I have 6 apples. How many more do I need to fill a box of 10?
Which pair makes 10?
If 0 + 10 = 10, then 10 + 0 = ?
questions.explanation: Even if we swap them, 10 + 0 is still 10!

questions.question: Count the stars to make 10: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ + ?
questions.explanation: We have 4 stars, we need 6 more to make 10!

questions.question: 1 + 9 makes 10. What about 2 + 8?
questions.explanation: 2 + 8 also makes 10! It's another set of partners.

questions.question: You have 10 sweets and you get 0 more. Do you still have 10?

modules.title: Addition Review & Mixed Practice
modules.description: Putting all our addition skills together for numbers up to 10.
modules.learningGoal: Confidently solve a variety of addition problems using different methods.

questions.question: What is 4 + 3?
Which one is a double? 🦁🦁 + 🦁🦁
questions.explanation: 2 + 2 is a double and it equals 4!

questions.question: If you are at 5 on the number line and jump 5 more, where do you land?
questions.explanation: 5 + 5 = 10. You reached the end of the line!
Adding zero changes nothing. It's still 8!

questions.question: Thandi has 3 pens. She finds 3 more. How many does she have?
Which pair makes 10?
Is 1 + 9 the same as 9 + 1?
questions.explanation: Yes! In addition, the order doesn't change the answer.

questions.question: Count the total: 🥥🥥🥥🥥🥥 + 🥥
You made it to the end! What is 5 + 2?

modules.title: Adding Three Numbers
modules.description: Learning to put three different groups together.
modules.learningGoal: Add three single-digit numbers with a sum up to 10.

questions.question: Count the fruit: 🍎 + 🍌 + 🍎
questions.explanation: One apple, one banana, and another apple makes 3 fruits!
5 + 0 is still 5, and one more is 6.

questions.question: 1 + 2 + 3 = 6. Is this true?
Which sum equals 10?

modules.title: Addition to 15
modules.description: Moving beyond ten into the teen numbers.
modules.learningGoal: Solve addition problems with sums between 11 and 15.

questions.question: Count the stars: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ + ⭐⭐⭐

questions.explanation: A full ten and a five makes 15.

questions.question: Which sum equals 15?

modules.title: Addition to 20
modules.description: The final challenge for Grade 1 addition.
modules.learningGoal: Add numbers with sums up to 20.

modules.title: School & Classroom Stories
modules.description: Solving problems about pens, books, and friends at school.
modules.learningGoal: Extract numbers from school-themed stories to find the total sum.

questions.question: A teacher has 3 red pens and 2 blue pens. How many pens does she have in total?
There are 4 boys and 4 girls sitting at a table. How many children are at the table?
questions.explanation: 4 + 4 is a double! That makes 8 children.

questions.question: Lindiwe has 6 library books. She borrows 1 more. How many books does she have now?
You have 2 pencils in your case. Your friend gives you 3 more. Do you have 5 pencils now?
The class has 5 soccer balls and 5 netball balls. How many balls are there altogether?
A lunch box has 2 sandwiches and 2 apples. How many items are in the lunch box?
There are 7 chairs in the room. The teacher brings in 3 more chairs. How many chairs are there now?
If Sipho has 1 ruler and buys 1 more, he has 3 rulers. Is this true?
One desk has 4 crayons. Another desk has 5 crayons. How many crayons in total?
3 friends are playing tag. 4 more friends join them. How many friends are playing now?

modules.title: Farm & Animal Stories
modules.description: Counting animals on a South African farm.
modules.learningGoal: Solve addition word problems using animal characters.

questions.question: A farm has 5 cows and 2 calves. How many cattle are there in total?
There are 4 chickens in the coop. 6 more chickens walk in. How many chickens are there?
A dog has 4 puppies. Another dog has 3 puppies. How many puppies are there altogether?
A pond has 8 frogs. 2 more jump in. How many frogs are in the pond?
If you see 2 sheep and 5 goats, there are 7 animals. Is this true?
There are 3 birds in a tree. 3 more birds land in the tree. How many birds are there?
A farmer picks 6 mielies in the morning and 3 in the afternoon. How many mielies does he have?
A cat catches 1 mouse. Later it catches 2 more. How many mice did it catch?
There are 7 ducks swimming. 1 more duck joins them. How many ducks are there?
A kraal has 4 sheep and 1 bull. How many animals are in the kraal?

modules.title: Market & Shopping Stories
modules.description: Using addition to buy fruit and treats.
modules.learningGoal: Solve addition word problems in a shopping context.

questions.question: Mom buys 2 oranges and 4 bananas. How many fruits does she have?
Sweets cost 5 Madibas XP and a chocolate costs 5 Madibas XP. How much in total?
You buy 7 apples. You go back and buy 3 more. How many apples do you have?
A bag has 8 potatoes. Dad adds 1 more potato. How many are in the bag?
If a juice is 4 coins and a bread is 4 coins, you need 8 coins. Is this true?
Lerato buys 3 green lollipops and 5 red lollipops. How many lollipops does she have?
A shop has 6 soccer jerseys. They get 2 new ones. How many jerseys do they have?
You have 9 stickers and buy 1 more. You now have 10. Is this true?
There are 4 boxes of milk on the shelf. The worker puts 4 more boxes. How many are there?
A man buys 2 packs of eggs. Each pack has 3 eggs. How many eggs in total?

modules.title: Missing Number Mysteries
modules.description: Finding the missing part of the sum to reach the total.
modules.learningGoal: Solve for the missing addend in equations up to 20.

questions.question: 8 + __ = 12. What is the missing number?
questions.explanation: Count on from 8: 9, 10, 11, 12. That is 4 steps!

questions.question: __ + 5 = 15. What number goes in the blank?
Double __ = 14. What number was doubled?
12 + __ = 12. What is the secret number?
questions.explanation: If the answer stays the same, you added zero!

questions.question: 14 + __ = 16. How many jumps do we need?
Is the missing number in '9 + __ = 18' the number 9?
6 + __ = 13. Find the missing friend.
11 + __ = 20. How much more to reach twenty?
If 10 + __ = 13, then the answer is 3. Is this true?
questions.explanation: Correct! Ten and three make thirteen.

questions.question: 15 + __ = 19. What is the gap?

modules.title: The Bridge Method (Crossing 10)
modules.description: Learning to break numbers apart to make a ten first.
modules.learningGoal: Understand how to 'Make 10' as a strategy for larger addition.

questions.question: To solve 8 + 5, we can do 8 + 2 + 3. What is the answer?
questions.explanation: 8 + 2 makes 10, then add the left-over 3 to get 13!

questions.question: What is 7 + 4? (Hint: Make 10 first)
9 + 6 is the same as 10 + 5. Is this true?
questions.explanation: Yes! You take 1 from the 6 to make the 9 a 10.

questions.question: Which sum is the easiest way to solve 9 + 4?
questions.explanation: Adding 1 to 9 makes 10, which is easier to count from!

questions.question: To solve 9 + 8, you make a 10 and add __.
questions.explanation: 9 + 1 = 10. You took 1 from the 8, so 7 are left. 10 + 7 = 17.

modules.title: Double Digits Plus Single Digits
modules.description: Adding numbers like 14 + 3 or 11 + 6.
modules.learningGoal: Add a single-digit number to a double-digit number up to 20.

questions.question: If you have 12 stickers and get 6 more, you have 18. True or False?

modules.title: Mental Math Sprinter
modules.description: Speedy addition using doubles and near-doubles up to 20.
modules.learningGoal: Quickly solve sums by recognizing patterns and doubles.

questions.question: What is double 9 plus 1? (9 + 9 + 1)
questions.explanation: Double 9 is 18, and one more is 19!

questions.question: If 7 + 7 = 14, what is 7 + 8?
questions.explanation: 7 + 8 is a near-double. It's just one more than 14!

questions.question: What is double 10?

questions.explanation: Double 6 is 12, then add 2 more to get 14.

questions.question: Is 8 + 9 the same as 17?
Double 5 plus double 5 is... ?
Which sum is the largest?
If you double 0, what do you get?
What is double 6 plus double 2?

modules.title: Three-Part Stories
modules.description: Complex word problems with three different items to add.
modules.learningGoal: Solve multi-step word problems within a single equation.

questions.question: Busi has 4 blue marbles, 3 red marbles, and 3 green marbles. How many in total?
A basket has 5 bananas, 5 apples, and 5 pears. How many fruits are there?
You see 2 lions, 2 zebras, and 6 monkeys. How many animals are there?
Mom buys 8 eggs, then 4 more, then 2 more. How many eggs now?
A garden has 7 red flowers, 1 yellow flower, and 7 white flowers. How many flowers total?
If you have 1 cat, 1 dog, and 1 bird, you have 3 pets. Is this true?
There are 6 cars, 6 trucks, and 0 bikes. How many vehicles are there?
Sipho finds 9 shells. He finds 2 more, then another 2. How many shells?
3 birds + 3 birds + 4 birds = 10 birds. True or False?
A box has 2 pencils, 8 crayons, and 5 pens. How many things are inside?

modules.title: Addition Mastery Test
modules.description: The ultimate mix of everything you've learned.
modules.learningGoal: Demonstrate mastery of all addition concepts up to 20.

questions.question: What is 2 more than double 8?
14 + __ = 20. Find the missing number.
Which makes 15? 7 + 8 OR 9 + 5?
Start at 13. Jump forward 5. Where are you?
If I have 10 and you have 7, how many do we have together?
Final question: What is 10 + 5 + 5?

modules.title: Taking Away from 5
modules.description: Learning how to subtract small numbers and see what's left.
modules.learningGoal: Subtract single-digit numbers from a total of 5 or less.

questions.question: If you have 2 apples and you eat 1, how many are left? 🍎🍎 - 🍎

questions.explanation: Take 1 away from 3. You have 2 left!
Hold up 5 fingers. Put 2 down. You have 3 fingers standing!

questions.question: If you have 4 sweets and you give 4 away, how many do you have left?
questions.explanation: 4 - 4 = 0. When you take everything away, nothing is left!
Taking away zero means nothing changes! You still have 2.

questions.question: 4 - 1 = 3. Is this true?
questions.explanation: Yes! If you have 4 and take 1 away, you have 3.

questions.question: Count the remaining stars: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ - ⭐⭐⭐
If I have 1 toy and I take away 1 toy, I have 0 toys. True or False?

modules.title: Subtracting from 10
modules.description: Practicing subtraction with numbers up to 10.
modules.learningGoal: Subtract single-digit numbers from a total of 10.

modules.title: Subtraction Stories
modules.description: Solving take-away problems in real life.
modules.learningGoal: Understand word problems that require subtraction.

modules.title: Taking Away from 5
modules.description: Learning how to subtract small numbers and see what's left.
modules.learningGoal: Subtract single-digit numbers from a total of 5 or less.

questions.question: If you have 2 apples and you eat 1, how many are left? 🍎🍎 - 🍎

questions.explanation: Take 1 away from 3. You have 2 left!
Hold up 5 fingers, put 2 down. You have 3 left!

questions.question: If you have 4 toys and give 1 to a friend, how many do you have? 🧸🧸🧸🧸 - 🧸

questions.explanation: If you have 5 and take all 5 away, you have zero left!
Taking away zero means nothing changes. You still have 3!

questions.question: If you have 5 birds and 1 flies away, how many stay? 🐦🐦🐦🐦🐦 - 🐦
Is 2 - 2 = 0?
questions.explanation: Yes! Any number minus itself is always zero.

modules.title: Subtracting from 10
modules.description: Practicing subtraction with numbers up to 10.
modules.learningGoal: Subtract single-digit numbers from a total of 10.

questions.explanation: If you have 10 and take 3 away, 7 are left.

questions.question: If you have 8 marbles and lose 2, how many do you have?

questions.explanation: Five is half of ten, so 10 - 5 = 5!

questions.question: You have 9 balloons and 4 pop 🎈. How many are left?

questions.explanation: If you take 3 away from 7, you have 4 left.

questions.question: Is 10 - 10 = 0?
questions.explanation: Yes! Taking everything away leaves zero.

questions.question: Start at 10 and count back 2. Where are you?
If you have 10 and take 0 away, do you still have 10?
questions.explanation: Correct! Zero away means no change.
If you take almost everything away, only 1 is left!

modules.title: Subtraction Story Problems
modules.description: Real-life scenarios where things go away.
modules.learningGoal: Identify subtraction keywords like 'left', 'lost', and 'gave away'.

questions.question: There are 6 birds in a tree. 4 fly away. How many birds stay?
You have 10 Madibas XP. You spend 5 on a sticker. How much is left?
A farmer has 8 cows. 3 walk into the barn. How many are still in the field?
You find 7 shells on the beach. You lose 2 in the sand. How many shells do you have now?
The baker makes 9 cupcakes. He sells 7. How many cupcakes are left in the shop?
There are 5 monkeys on a branch. All 5 jump off. How many monkeys are left?
A bus starts with 10 people. 4 people get off at the first stop. How many stay on the bus?
Sarah had 6 crayons. She gave 3 to her brother. How many does Sarah have left?
You have 8 slices of pizza. Your friends eat 6 slices. How many slices are left for you?
10 zebras were at the water hole. 2 ran away. How many zebras are left?

modules.title: The 'Difference' Detective
modules.description: Comparing two groups to find the difference.
modules.learningGoal: Use subtraction to compare quantities.

questions.question: Thabo has 9 cars. Sarah has 6 cars. How many more cars does Thabo have?
Box A has 10 pencils. Box B has 7. What is the difference?
An elephant eats 10 buns. A monkey eats 4 buns. How many more buns did the elephant eat?
Team Gold has 8 points. Team Green has 5 points. How many points ahead is Team Gold?
A blue pencil is 10cm long. A red pencil is 8cm long. How much longer is the blue one?
Lerato has 7 stickers. Sipho has 7 stickers. What is the difference?
There are 9 girls and 5 boys in a class. How many more girls are there?
A cat weighs 5kg. A dog weighs 9kg. How much less does the cat weigh?
Bongi scored 10 goals. Tumi scored 2 goals. How many fewer goals did Tumi score?
One tree has 6 apples. Another has 1 apple. How many more apples are on the first tree?

modules.title: Zero Heroes & Subtracting All
modules.description: Understanding what happens with 0 and total subtraction.
modules.learningGoal: Master the concept of subtracting 0 and subtracting a number from itself.

questions.question: Match the problems to their answers!
questions.explanation: Great! When you subtract everything, you get 0. When you subtract nothing, it stays the same!
If you have 7 and take all 7 away, you have 0 left.

questions.question: If you have 10 Madibas XP and spend 0, how much do you have left?
questions.explanation: Since you spent nothing, your total stays the same!
Subtracting a number from itself always results in zero.
Even with small numbers, taking away zero leaves the number exactly as it was.

questions.question: You have 6 apples and eat all 6. How many are left?
8 - 0 = 8. Is this true?
questions.explanation: True! Subtracting zero does not decrease the value.
When the numbers are the same, the difference is zero.

questions.question: There are 5 birds. 0 fly away. How many stay?
3 - 3 = 0. Is this true?
